metadata.datasetName: Sensor-free Physical and Brain Readiness Activity Map
metadata.importantLabel: Estimated readiness, not measured body or brain energy
metadata.medicalDisclaimer: This heuristic is not a diagnostic, medical, occupational-safety, or fitness-clearance tool. It cannot detect illness, sleep disorders, dehydration, hypoglycemia, injury, heat illness, or dangerous fatigue.
metadata.method: Activity MET/intensity estimates inform physical drain. Duration, cognitive load, stress, recovery and mood inform brain-readiness change. Age multipliers are deliberately small because fitness, health, sleep quality and individual response usually matter more than age alone.
sourceBasis: 2024 Adult Compendium of Physical Activities (standardized MET values; adults 19–59)
2024 Older Adult Compendium of Physical Activities (older-adult activity costs)
WHO Guidelines on Physical Activity and Sedentary Behaviour (2020)
CDC About Sleep: recommended sleep duration changes by age (2024)
CDC/NIOSH Fatigue and Work: fatigue relates to sleep disruption, long/irregular hours, stress, mental/physical demand and heat (2026)
accuracyNotes: No universal world-average brain-energy score exists.
The same activity can energize one person and fatigue another.
Use confidence bands and personalize with optional one-tap self-ratings after activities.
Do not compare scores between users as if they were laboratory measurements.

ageBands.note: Use parent/guardian input; activity estimates are especially uncertain for children.
School load and growth make sleep particularly important.
Small conservative adjustment only; fitness matters more than age alone.
Increase physical drain slightly for vigorous/manual activities.
Use lower confidence unless mobility and fitness are known.
Large individual variation; do not use for safety-critical decisions.

activities.name: Video call with friends or family
